metadata.metaTitle: Recycle St. Louis | Metro High School Advocacy
metadata.metaDescription: 0 curbside pickups. 14 drop-off sites. 100% our responsibility. Help Metro High students educate STL.

popup.message: 🚨 Important Update: Residential recycling is no longer being picked up in many STL neighborhoods. To ensure your items are recycled, they must be taken to an official drop-off station.

hero.title: Recycle St. Louis
hero.tagline: A student advocacy project bringing awareness to the state of recycling in our city.

interview.label: Exclusive Interview
interview.title: Talking Policy with Mayor Cara Spencer
interview.description: We sat down with St. Louis Mayor Cara Spencer to discuss the suspension of curbside recycling and what the city's long-term plan is for sustainable waste management.

article.title: The Curbside Crisis
article.body: Due to labor shortages and high contamination rates, St. Louis has officially pivoted to a 'Drop-Off' model. Placing recyclables in your alley bin now almost guarantees they end up in a landfill.

mapPage.metaDescription: Find the 14 official recycling drop-off stations in St. Louis city.
mapPage.title: Find a Station
mapPage.tagline: Official St. Louis City recycling drop-off locations. Use this map to find the station nearest to your neighborhood.
mapPage.instructionsBody: Click on the icons above to view the specific address and accepted materials for each location. Most sites are open 24/7, but please ensure your items are clean and sorted according to the posted signs to prevent contamination.

interviewsPage.metaDescription: Watch our exclusive sit-downs with city leaders and experts regarding the future of waste management in St. Louis.
interviewsPage.title: Interviews
interviewsPage.tagline: Voices of the city: exploring the policy, the problems, and the potential solutions for our environment.
